data.title: Shell: Improved Production Prospects And Outlook (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
Oil and gas company Shell plc (NYSE:SHEL) (OTCPK:RYDAF) didn't impress investors with its Q2 2025 and H1 2025 results earlier today. The stock was slightly down, by 2%, in today's trading session immediately following the results.

There's a good reason for this. The earnings were expectedly weak and are expected to remain so for the remainder of 2025 as well. However, there's a lot more going for Shell now compared to where it was the last I checked. Here, I discuss the latest earnings and other developments, which tilt the balance towards a Buy rating.

Earnings see sharp drop

The company continued to see a drop in revenues in Q2 2025. After declining by 4.5% QoQ in Q1 2025, the number dropped further by 5.5% YoY in Q2. There's no solace to be found in the YoY figures either, with the revenue having fallen by 8.4% YoY in H1 2025.

The weakness in revenues is reflected in an earnings decline as well. Adjusted earnings fell by 24% QoQ in Q2 2025, which is a particular disappointment after the number actually saw a big increase of 52% QoQ in Q1 2025. Previous quarter's earnings jumped due to a favorable base effect, though, as Q4 2024 was affected by massive levies and impairments.

At the same time, there's no denying the genuine decline in the latest quarter either. This is apparent even in the YoY change in earnings. While in Q1 2025 the number was down by 28% YoY, it's now down by an even bigger 30% YoY in H1 2025.

The reason behind the weak results is no puzzle either. The company's production guidance for gas, upstream and marketing, all pointed towards a weak Q2 2025 compared with Q1 2025. It didn't help that oil prices were lackluster during the quarter, and gas prices also lost steam compared to Q1 2025.

Better production expected...

If production held Shell back in Q2 2025, however, it can give it a push forward in the next quarter as well. The guidance certainly suggests so. As the tables below show, both integrated gas and upstream production are expected to see improvements at the midpoint of the guidance ranges next quarter.

LNG liquefaction volumes are expected to see the biggest increase of ~5% QoQ, while integrated gas production is expected to rise by 2.9% QoQ. Upstream production too, is expected to see a 3.9% QoQ increase.

The only exception to the positive outlook is the forecast for marketing sales volumes, which reflect a 2.2% drop at the midpoint. Here too, though, there's a possibility of an increase, though, if the figure comes in towards the top end of the guidance range.

...but price support remains to be seen

Whether oil and gas prices support the forecast production uptick, however, remains to be seen. The US's Energy Information Agency [EIA] expects Brent crude to drop by 14.8% in 2025 to USD 69/bbl from last year. It's more optimistic on gas prices though. Even after some reduction in forecast since the last I checked, EIA projects a 1.68x YoY increase.

Assuming that Shell maintains its production guidance, at the very least, it's likely that the extent of reduction in its revenues and earnings can slow down in Q3 2025. Or do better than that, going by analysts' estimates on Seeking Alpha.

Revenues are projected to be at USD 70.2 billion during the quarter, and earnings per ADR are estimated at USD 1.55. These indicate increases of 7.3% QoQ and 7.6% QoQ respectively, even as both figures still represent YoY declines.

Upgraded outlook and solid returns

In fact, it's worth pointing out that the full year 2025 estimates also pencil in a small increase in revenues in 2025 compared with the expectation of a contraction by 5.75% the last time I checked. That said, earnings are still expected to drop by over 20%, continuing the trend already seen.

Despite the drop in earnings, though, the company's dividend payout ratio continues to look alright at 43.7%. If Shell maintains its dividends at the present levels for the remainder of the year, it forward dividend yield comes to 3.95%, which is at its five-year average level.

The company's buybacks are also expected to continue. After repurchasing shares worth USD 3.5 billion in Q2 2025, it expects to repeat the process with the same amount in Q3 as well.

Market multiples remain elevated

The big downer for SHEL remains the high non-GAAP forward P/E ratio at 11.95x. This compares unfavorably with the stock's five-year average of 10.1x, indicating a 15% downside. At the same time, for an investor with a medium term perspective in mind, the stock still looks good (see chart below).

In any case, the real value of even passive returns on the stock is only realized over at least the medium term. Consider this. Over a one year period, total returns for SHEL have been 4.1 percentage points [pp] higher than price returns. But the number jumps to 17.4 pp over a three year time frame.

What next?

The latest results continue to reflect ongoing weakness in Shell's financials. With the outlook for oil prices soft for 2025, there's a question mark on whether the company can show improved performance going forward either.

That said, there are also multiple factors working for it. Gas prices, for example, are expected to be stronger YoY. This outlook is supported by improved production expectations for Q3 2025. Even analysts' expectations of the full-year revenue number are mildly positive now. Additionally, there's no risk to dividends, and buybacks are a nice touch too.

Even though the stock's forward P/E is high, there are still gains to be made for the medium term investor, especially considering passive returns over a three year time frame. The stock's forward P/E also looks more reasonable from 2027 onwards. In a nutshell, SHEL is a good buy for medium-term investors now. I'm upgrading it to Buy.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: SoFi Stock Is Stumbling; Here's A Strong Buy Alternative
data.description:
So Long, SoFi Rally

SoFi (SOFI) investors were taken on an emotional rollercoaster ride this week.

On Tuesday, the digital-first “neobank” notched another blockbuster quarter, reporting Q2 revenue of $858M (a 43.7% year-over-year increase) that beat Wall Street’s expectations by $53.64M. The San Francisco-based fintech anticipates adjusted net revenue of roughly $3.4B—$65M higher than the top of its previous guidance.

"SoFi’s Q2 spotlight was the 72% Y/Y surge in fee-based revenue, now making up 44% of total revenue, a clear signal it's shifting from a lender to a platform-first business," said Seeking Alpha analyst Yiannis Zourmpanos. "This structural mix shift unlocks margin scalability and de-risks the model."

SOFI shares rose nearly 18% at Tuesday’s market open following the blockbuster Q2 2025 earnings…that is, until the company issued an end-of-day announcement detailing a $1.5B common stock offering, erasing much of the previous session’s gains in after-hours trading.

So why the sudden change-up? The share offering would represent around 6% of SOFI’s current market cap, sparking investor anxieties over dilution of existing shares.Many investors are unperturbed by the dilution dilemma. As SA analyst The Asian Investor wrote on Wednesday:

SoFi Technologies delivered a strong Q2 report, crushing earnings and revenue estimates, fueled by impressive customer growth and robust financial services expansion. Despite a 6% share drop on a $1.5B stock sale, I see this capital raise as an opportunity for additional growth and investments, not a reason to sell.

Others have taken SOFI’s rapidly growing share price as a sign of an increasingly frothy valuation. As SA analyst JR Research writes:

SoFi delivered another stellar quarter, consistently beating estimates and showing robust revenue and earnings growth, possibly impressing even the skeptics…The valuation is extremely stretched at 61x forward EPS, suggesting years of optimism are already priced in, leaving little room for disappointment.

SA Quant’s SOFI Assessment

SOFI is a digital bank and fintech platform offering various services, including loan origination, online banking, insurance, and investing services. It acquired cloud payments platform Galileo Financial Technologies in 2020 for $1.2B, which now powers its “Technology Platform” unit, helping corporate clients launch digital bank accounts, issue debit and credit cards, and more.

SoFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: SOFI)

  • Market Capitalization: $24.94B

  • Sector: Financials

  • Industry: Consumer Finance

  • Quant Rating: Hold

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/31/25): 117 out of 692

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/31/25): 12 out of 38

SA’s Quant system currently assigns SOFI a Hold rating, which has remained unchanged over the past year due to the digital lender’s low profitability score. Earning an overall ‘D-’ in the category, data points including SOFI’s -$1.84B cash from operations TTM and $1.91 in cash per share TTM (a -72% difference from the broader financial sector) weigh on its overall Quant rating.

SOFI Quant Summary (as of 7/31/25)

However, both SOFI’s valuation and momentum have improved significantly. Its valuation grade has improved from a ‘D’ to a ‘B-’ over the past three months, boosted higher by a forward non-GAAP PEG ratio of 1x, a 20.3% discount from the sector median.

SOFI’s momentum grade has consistently hovered between an ‘A-’ and an ‘A’ since mid-October 2024. That marks a dramatic improvement from early October, when its momentum grade oscillated between a ‘D-’ and a ‘D+’.

SOFI Quant Rating History, October 2024

Market-crushing momentum metrics, including SOFI’s one-year price performance of 205.6%, a 2,666.5% difference from the 7.4% sector median, support its ‘A+ momentum grade today.

SOFI also demonstrates impressive growth, with forward revenue growth of 24.2%, a 262.6% difference from the 6.7% sector median. Long-term EPS growth of 79% is a 663.2% difference from the 10.3% sector median.

As SA’s Quant data shows, SOFI offers impressive growth for a reasonable price compared to many of its financial sector rivals. However, its low profitability and newly unveiled share sale could put off certain investors. SA’s Quant Screener can help identify higher-rated alternatives exhibiting equally impressive growth momentum at reasonable prices.

Identifying Strong Buy Financials With SA’s Screening Tool

A few weeks ago, I demonstrated how to use SA’s screening tool to identify top-tier U.S. semiconductor stocks as potential alternatives to Nvidia (NVDA) after the chipmaking heavyweight hit a historic $4T market cap milestone. Today, let’s use it to identify high-performance financial stocks exhibiting equally strong performance to SOFI and better profitability. Using SA Quant’s custom screening tool, you can filter for the following categories:

  • Sector: Financials

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Market Cap: $600M or greater

You can also use SA’s Top Financial Stocks screener as a starting point.

As of July 30, 2025, SA’s Quant system identified 56 financial stocks with Strong Buy ratings and market caps greater than $600M, some of which appeared in my recent pieces:

  • Bank On It: Top Financial Stocks For Earnings Season

  • Value Vacation: Five Top Emerging Market Financials

  • Returns Made In China: Three Strong Buy Financials.

Let’s explore one of the top 10 highest-ranked stocks within these parameters that remains one of my favorite financial picks: FinVolution Group (FINV).

FINV Quant Summary (as of 7/31/25)

FinVolution Group (NYSE: FINV)

  • Market Capitalization: $2.21B

  • Sector: Financials

  • Industry: Consumer Finance

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/31/2025): 10 out of 692

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/31/2025): 3 out of 38

A member of the PRO Quant Portfolio and one of my Top 10 Stocks For H2 2025, FinVolution Group (FINV) is a consumer lending platform that connects borrowers and businesses across China and other parts of Asia. Like SOFI, the Chinese fintech has become a top player in consumer loans, exhibiting fantastic momentum and sector-leading growth. With an overall ‘A-’ profitability grade, however, it boasts a far healthier balance sheet.

FINV is in a strong position following excellent Q1 2025 results. Revenues increased 9.4% year-over-year, while transaction volume rose 7.9% Y/Y. Its upward climb has benefited from strong growth tailwinds, including China’s push to boost domestic lenders as the government looks to ease regulation in the credit sector.

As SA analyst Veltri Capital Insights recently wrote:

FinVolution demonstrates strong revenue growth, expanding margins, healthy liquidity, and consistent shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The stock trades at a significant discount to peers, with my DCF model indicating high upside potential over the next decade.

FINV is also a far better bargain than SOFI, boasting an ‘A+’ valuation grade vs. the neobank’s ‘B-’ valuation grade. Its forward GAAP P/E of 5.3 is nearly a 54.8% discount to the sector median, while its forward EV/EBITDA ratio of 2.15 is over a 81% discount to the 11.3 sector median.

The company also earns an ‘A’ in momentum, with one-year price performance of 60.2%, a 709.9% difference from the 7.4% sector median. Growth has steadily improved over the past three months from a ‘C+’ to a ‘B’, boosted by a forward free cash flow per share growth rate of 61.1%, a 376.2% difference from the 12.8% sector median.

As discussed, FINV’s profitability ‘A-’ Quant Factor Grade eclipses that of SOFI’s, with a return on total assets TTM of 10.65%, an 831.6% difference to the 1.14% sector median.

Conclusion: SA Quant’s Strong Buys > SOFI

SOFI’s fantastic second-quarter performance, incredible momentum, solid growth, and sector-leading PEG ratio are a few of the factors that could make it an attractive investment for those with solid risk appetites. However, SA’s Quant System shows plenty of higher-rated financial sector alternatives with stronger fundamentals and robust balance sheets. You can use SA’s Quant system customizable screening tool to explore the whole universe of financial stocks through an unbiased lens.

Alternatively, consider joining our Alpha Picks community to receive two new Quant Strong Buy portfolio additions monthly, with real-time sell alerts when ratings shift and detailed performance analysis.

data.title: What Options Data May Indicate About Mag 7 Earnings
data.description:
As earnings season heats up, market participants are closely watching the Magnificent Seven (Mag 7) stocks for signs of volatility and directional momentum. With upcoming earnings reports, options pricing reveals heightened expectations for post-earnings moves.

Heavyweights Set to Drive Market Volume and Volatility

Six of the seven Mag 7 companies reported earnings at the end of July - Google (GOOG, GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) reported on July 23, followed by Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN) the following week. Nvidia (NVDA) does not report earnings until late August.

Options on the Mag 7 make up about 25% of the single stock option volume daily. There are 8 million Mag 7 options contracts traded per day, equating to about $5 billon in premium. In total, Mag 7 stocks trade half a billion shares per day, or 4% of spot volume for single stocks. The cohort is popular with retail traders because of their performance, media coverage, volatility, and opportunities for leverage.

How Options Pricing Reveals Earnings Expectations

Immediately ahead of an earnings release, short-dated options prices reflect the market expectations of how a stock is likely to react. The simplest way to estimate the magnitude of the reaction is by comparing the straddle price to historic moves. Mathematically, a comparison between near-term volatility and longer-dated volatility results in the expected standard deviation for the post-earnings day. Note that options pricing generally does not imply the direction of a reaction - just how much the stock is expected to move in either direction. In some cases, positioning and put/call skew can add some context to expectations.

A quick look at how traders are pricing in potential upcoming Mag 7 moves shows standard deviations based on implied volatility, converted into an expected move using a 2/3 rule of thumb to estimate the highest-probability point.

Traders Temper Reactions Ahead of Earnings

Generally, these single stock options price in moves slightly below historical averages, suggesting traders are not expecting any major impact to the stock price based on earnings results. Including Netflix (NFLX) in the analysis gives us a recent point of comparison, with a downward reaction that was generally in line with the implied expectation.

In terms of direction, it’s a mixed bag for these stocks historically, however, there does seem to be some clustering, where most of the stocks move in the same direction in each quarter.

For options traders, earnings periods can present opportunities, but the potential for large gap moves introduces greater risk. Understanding the data and historical behavior is crucial to managing expectations and making informed decisions, serving as a useful tool for any strategy.

Disclaimer:

Before engaging in any transactions in those products, it is important for market participants to carefully review the disclosures and disclaimers contained at: https://www.cboe.com/us_disclaimers/ . These products are complex and are suitable only for sophisticated market participants. In certain jurisdictions, Cboe Company products are only permitted for investment professionals, certified sophisticated investors, or high net worth corporations and associations.
